_meta.author: scorecard-refine (national autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House HD104-108 batch: Jessica Gonzalez (D-104), Terry Meza (D-105), Jared Patterson (R-106), Linda Garcia (D-107), Morgan Meyer (R-108). All sitting incumbents; state tier. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. High-value catches: Morgan Meyer was the LONE Republican to vote AGAINST HB 1927 constitutional carry (2021) -> 2A self_defense[0] FALSE (corrects a prior 10/10); Meyer carries an Empower Texans/TFR 'F' fiscal rating ('Fiscal Foe') -> economic[2] FALSE; Jared Patterson voted to weaken the foreign-farmland-ownership ban (SB17 Amd#4, 2025) -> border[4] FALSE.

jessica-gonzalez@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Jessica Gonzalez, D, HD-104 (Dallas), attorney, co-founder/vice-chair of the Texas House LGBTQ+ Caucus, former vice-chair House Elections Committee; in office since 2019. Documented progressive on every researched issue: pro-choice (voted against SB 8 / HB 1280; Planned Parenthood Texas Votes + Annie's List endorsed); LGBTQ-caucus leader who opposed SB 14 (trans-minor procedure ban); voucher opponent (Texas AFT endorsed, voted for budget rider barring private-school voucher funds); led the 2021 House Democratic walkout that temporarily killed the SB 1/SB 7 election bill; Empower Texans/TFR fiscal index grade F (score 27, 2023). No cross-party (conservative) deviation found. Cells with no public position (life of unborn beyond abortion, religious-liberty conscience bills, 10th-Amendment, border roll-calls, 2A votes) left null. Running unopposed in the 2026 Democratic primary, no GOP general challenger.

0.note: Pro-choice; on record 'I will always defend the right to access safe legal abortion care.' Contrary to personhood-from-conception.

1.note: Voted AGAINST SB 8 (6-week ban) and HB 1280; offered amendments to weaken restrictions. Opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed/backed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes PAC and Annie's List (abortion-rights PAC).

0.note: Co-founder/vice-chair of the Texas House LGBTQ+ Caucus; does not affirm marriage as one man/one woman.

1.note: LGBTQ-rights leader; opposed SB 1978; supports same-sex marriage.

2.note: Opposed SB 14 (ban on transgender medical procedures for minors); raised points of order against it on the floor.

4.note: Endorsed by Stonewall Democrats of Dallas; advocates for LGBTQ identity in public policy/schools.

0.note: Voucher opponent; voted for a budget amendment barring state funds for private-school vouchers; Texas AFT endorsed. Opposes school choice.

2.note: Empower Texans/TFR fiscal index grade F (score 27, 2023; career F) — documented high-spending voting record.

1.note: Led/joined the 2021 Democratic walkout to kill SB 1/SB 7; pushed amendments to weaken its voter-ID and verification provisions.

2.note: Signature priority of expanding voting access; opposite of single-day in-person voting with limited absentee.

3.note: Opposed SB 1, which restricted mail voting, drop boxes, and ballot harvesting.

terry-meza@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Terry Meza, D, HD-105 (Irving, Dallas County); continuous service since Jan 2019. Documented progressive: Texas Right to Life 2019 score 0%, Texas Alliance for Life 2025 50% (credited on non-restrictive bereavement/palliative-care items, not abortion limits), Planned Parenthood Texas Votes endorsed; Equality Texas endorsed and authored a pro-equality bill; voted NAY on SB 2 (2025 ESA vouchers, Vote Smart) and is a stated voucher opponent (Texas AFT endorsed); Empower Texans/TFR fiscal index lifetime F. Gun-control record: authored HB 196 adding a duty to retreat / weakening stand-your-ground, and her own priorities page calls for documenting handgun sales, closing the 'gun-show loophole,' and gun-violence-prevention research (Gun Owners of America labels her an anti-gun activist and endorsed her opponent). NOTE: the viral 'protect burglars' quotes attributed to her were FABRICATED satire (PolitiFact 'Pants on Fire'); not used as evidence. 'Build bridges not walls' on immigration. No conservative cross-party deviation. Running unopposed in the 2026 Democratic primary.

0.note: Texas Right to Life 2019 score 0%; pro-choice voting record. Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted against SB 22 (no taxpayer funding for abortion) and for amendments weakening it. Opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes.

0.note: Equality Texas endorsed; authored a pro-equality bill (86th). Does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: Equality Texas / Texas Equity PAC endorsed; pro-LGBTQ-rights record.

2.note: Voted against SB 14 (trans-minor procedure ban); not among the four Democrats who crossed over to support it. Supports prohibiting insurance exclusion of conversion-therapy coverage.

4.note: Equality Texas endorsed; pro-LGBTQ public-policy record.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB 2 (2025 ESA/vouchers); priorities page opposes vouchers that defund public schools.

2.note: Empower Texans/TFR fiscal index lifetime F (2025 score 25); supports accepting ~$100B federal Medicaid expansion. Documented high-spending record.

0.note: Own priorities page: 'Build bridges and not walls.' Opposite of supporting a completed physical border barrier.

1.note: Gun-safety priorities: document handgun sales, close the 'gun-show loophole,' fund gun-violence-prevention research; Gun Owners of America anti-endorsement. Contrary to opposing registries/limits.

3.note: Authored HB 196 adding a duty to retreat outside the home and removing automatic deadly-force justification for robbery off one's property — weakens stand-your-ground/castle doctrine.

jared-patterson@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Jared Patterson, R, HD-106 (Denton County); in office since 2019. Strongly conservative on life, sexuality, family, religious liberty, elections, sovereignty, and guns: Texas Right to Life 100% / Texas Alliance for Life 93% (voted for SB 8 Heartbeat Act); authored HB 1181 porn age-verification (upheld by SCOTUS 2025) and HB 900 READER Act; supported SB 14, HB 229 'What is a Woman Act,' SB 1257; co-authored SB 2 (2025 universal ESA/school choice) and voted yes; Texas Values 'Faith & Family Champion' 100% (SB 10 Ten Commandments, SB 11 prayer, SB 965 Coach Kennedy); voted SB 1 (2021) and authored SJR 37 citizenship-to-vote; voted HB 796 Texas Sovereignty Act and SB 1070 ERIC withdrawal; co-authored HB 1927 constitutional carry. DOCUMENTED DEVIATION (high value): he was CENSURED by the Republican Party of Texas (2025); among the censure items, he voted for SB 17 Amendment #4 (5/8/2025) WEAKENING the foreign-adversary farmland-ownership ban -> border[4] FALSE. Other censure flags (Texas Future Fund spending, film incentives, SB 2753 election-implementation delay, HR 4 rules) noted but did not cleanly negate scored cells. Won the 2026 GOP primary; faces a Democrat in November.

0.note: Texas Right to Life 100%, Texas Alliance for Life 93%; pro-life champion affirming life from conception.

1.note: Voted for SB 8 Heartbeat Act and supports Texas's near-total abortion ban (TRTL 100%).

2.note: Supported SB 14 (trans-minor procedure ban), HB 229 'What is a Woman Act,' SB 1257 Detransitioners Bill of Rights; affirms biological sex.

4.note: Authored HB 900 READER Act restricting sexually explicit school-library content; supported SB 13 (ban harmful books); voted NO on HB 1738 (kept homosexual-conduct statute).

0.note: Co-author of SB 2 (2025 universal ESA/school choice); voted yes.

1.note: Supported SB 12 Parents' Bill of Rights (parental rights over minors).

2.note: Voted for SB 17 (2023 DEI ban); authored HB 900 READER Act against explicit K-12 materials.

3.note: Authored HB 1181 porn age-verification law (upheld by SCOTUS 2025).

0.note: Supported SB 11 (prayer/Bible reading in schools) and SB 965 (Coach Kennedy religious-liberty law).

1.note: Supported SB 965 protecting religious expression by school employees (conscience/religious liberty).

3.note: Supported SB 10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms) and SB 11 (school prayer).

1.note: Voted for SB 1 (2021); authored SJR 37 (citizenship-to-vote) and co-authored HB 1243 (illegal-voting felony penalty).

3.note: Voted for SB 1 (2021), which restricted mail voting, drop boxes, and ballot harvesting.

0.note: Voted for HB 796 (2025 Texas Sovereignty Act) — 10th-Amendment nullification/anti-commandeering.

2.note: Voted for HB 796 (sovereignty) and SB 1070 (ERIC withdrawal) — resisting federal/multistate overreach on elections.

3.note: Voted for HB 796 Texas Sovereignty Act affirming state sovereignty.

4.note: Voted for SB 1070 withdrawing Texas from ERIC — opposing outside capture of state election data.

0.note: Supported the 2023 border-security package (HB 4 barrier funding, SB 4, HB 6).

4.note: DEVIATION: voted for SB 17 Amendment #4 (5/8/2025) weakening the ban on foreign-adversary (e.g. Chinese-national) ownership of Texas land — cited in his RPT censure. Contrary to opposing foreign ownership of US farmland.

0.note: Co-authored HB 1927 (2021 constitutional/permitless carry) and voted for it.

linda-garcia@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Linda Garcia, D, HD-107 (Dallas County, Garland/Mesquite); freshman (assumed office Jan 2025), succeeded Victoria Neave Criado; only the 89th (2025) session as a record and ran unopposed, so the public record is thin. Documented progressive anchors: campaign site states support for abortion access ('Reproductive Rights' priority); endorsed by Human Rights Campaign and Stonewall Democrats of Dallas (LGBTQ); voted NO on SB 2 (2025 vouchers) with all present Democrats and her site opposes vouchers (Texas AFT/TSTA endorsed). Most other issue areas (religious liberty, elections, public justice, 10th Amendment, border roll-calls, 2A) have no individually attributable vote or statement -> left null (not inferred from party). DATA NOTE for a human follow-up: the existing profile lists the wrong X/Twitter handle (@RepSylviaGarcia = US Rep Sylvia Garcia, a different person; correct is @lindafortexas) and an irrelevant 'Tennessee State Rifle Association' source — both should be corrected/removed. Running unopposed in the 2026 Democratic primary.

0.note: Campaign site: supports abortion access; lists 'Reproductive Rights' as a top priority. Contrary to personhood from conception.

1.note: Advocates abortion access — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Endorsed by Human Rights Campaign and Stonewall Democrats of Dallas; does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: HRC / Stonewall Democrats endorsement — pro-same-sex-marriage stance.

2.note: HRC endorsement (pro-transgender-rights organization) — does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: HRC / Stonewall Democrats endorsed — supports LGBTQ identity in public policy.

0.note: Voted NO on SB 2 (2025 ESA/vouchers) with all present Democrats; campaign opposes vouchers (Texas AFT/TSTA endorsed).

morgan-meyer@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Morgan Meyer, R, HD-108 (Dallas, Highland Park/University Park); in office since 2015, Chair of House Ways & Means. Conservative on life, sexuality (sex-definition), family/school choice, religious liberty, and public justice: Texas Alliance for Life 2025 100%, Texas Right to Life PAC endorsed, voted for SB 8 (2025 privacy act); voted for HB 229 'What is a Woman Act'; voted for SB 2 (2025 vouchers), SB 12 Parents' Bill of Rights, SB 13 (ban harmful books); voted for SB 10 (Ten Commandments) and SB 11 (school prayer); Texas District & County Attorneys 'Law & Order Award,' authored a violent-offender database and human-trafficking laws. TWO DOCUMENTED HIGH-VALUE DEVIATIONS: (1) 2A -- he was the LONE Republican to vote AGAINST HB 1927 (2021 constitutional carry) and the only R to vote for an amendment to kill it -> self_defense[0] FALSE (this CORRECTS a prior published 10/10 on 2A); (2) Fiscal -- Empower Texans/TFR fiscal index 'F' grades 2015-2023 (88th score 36, tied lowest-ranking Republican, labeled 'Fiscal Foe'; improved to 61 in 2025), plus Chapter 313 corporate-subsidy authorship -> economic[2] FALSE. Also a deviation: voted for HB 1738 (2025) repealing the homosexual-conduct statute, against Texas Values' position (still scored 86% with them). Election-integrity / border / 10th-Amendment cells from the prior profile were NOT corroborated by primary roll-call evidence -> reset to null. Won the 2026 GOP primary; faces a Democrat in November.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 2025 100%; Texas Right to Life PAC endorsed; voted for SB 8 (2025 privacy act). Pro-life.

1.note: Consistent pro-life voting record (TAL 100%) in a near-total-ban state.

4.note: Texas Right to Life PAC endorsed; no abortion-industry funding.

2.note: Voted for HB 229 'What is a Woman Act' (biological-sex definition).

4.note: Voted for SB 12 Parents' Bill of Rights and SB 13 (ban harmful/sexualized school-library books). NOTE deviation: also voted for HB 1738 repealing the homosexual-conduct statute, against Texas Values.

0.note: Voted for SB 2 (2025 ESA/school choice) — notable for a Dallas-area Republican.

1.note: Voted for SB 12 Parents' Bill of Rights (parental rights over minors).

2.note: Voted for SB 13 (2025 ban on harmful/sexualized content in school libraries).

0.note: Voted for SB 11 (prayer/Bible-reading time in schools).

3.note: Voted for SB 10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms) and SB 11 (school prayer).

2.note: DEVIATION: Empower Texans/TFR fiscal index 'F' 2015-2023 (88th score 36, tied lowest-ranking Republican, labeled 'Fiscal Foe'); Chapter 313 corporate-subsidy authorship. Documented high-spending record contrary to fiscal restraint.

0.note: Texas District & County Attorneys Association 'Law & Order Award'; affirmatively pro-law-enforcement record.

2.note: Coauthored violent-offender database legislation and human-trafficking laws — supports proportional punishment of evildoers / victim protection.

0.note: DEVIATION (high value): Morgan Meyer was the LONE Republican to vote AGAINST HB 1927 (2021 constitutional/permitless carry) and the only R to vote for an amendment to kill it. Corrects a prior published TRUE on constitutional carry.
